perturbation.original_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall terminate, with respect to any particular portion of NYNEX Confidential Information which: (a) was in CONSULTANT's possession, free of any obligation of confidence, prior to receipt from NYNEX, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; provided, however, that CONSULTANT immediately informs NYNEX, in writing, to establish its prior possession; (b) is already in the public domain at the time NYNEX communicates it to CONSULTANT, or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by CONSULTANT; (c) is received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to CONSULTANT; (d) is developed by CONSULTANT, independently of and without reference to any Confidential Information of NYNEX or any other information that NYNEX has disclosed in confidence to any third party, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; (e) is disclosed by CONSULTANT to a third party, with the express prior written permission of NYNEX; (f) is disclosed by CONSULTANT in order to satisfy any legal requirement of any competent government body; provided, however, that immediately upon CONSULTANT's receipt of any such request, CONSULTANT shall first advise NYNEX of same before making any disclosure to such body, so that NYNEX may either interpose an objection to such disclosure before such body, or take action to assure confidential handling of the Confidential Information by such body, or take other action to protect the Confidential Information which NYNEX deems appropriate under the circumstances; or (g) in any event, five (5) years after the date of execution of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall terminate, with respect to any particular portion of NYNEX Confidential Information which: (a) was in CONSULTANT's possession, free of any obligation of confidence, prior to receipt from NYNEX, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; provided, however, that CONSULTANT immediately informs NYNEX, in writing, to establish its prior possession; (b) is already in the public domain at the time NYNEX communicates it to CONSULTANT, or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by CONSULTANT; (c) is received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to CONSULTANT; (d) is developed by CONSULTANT, independently of and without reference to any Confidential Information of NYNEX or any other information that NYNEX has disclosed in confidence to any third party, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; (e) is disclosed by CONSULTANT to a third party, with the express prior written permission of NYNEX; (f) is disclosed by CONSULTANT in order to satisfy any legal requirement of any competent government body; provided, however, that immediately upon CONSULTANT's receipt of any such request, CONSULTANT shall first advise NYNEX of same before making any disclosure to such body, so that NYNEX may either interpose an objection to such disclosure before such body, or take action to assure confidential handling of the Confidential Information by such body, or take other action to protect the Confidential Information which NYNEX deems appropriate under the circumstances; or (g) in any event, ten (10) years after the date of execution of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the termination timeframe from five years to ten years creates an in-text contradiction with section 7, where it mentions that 'Notwithstanding any such termination, the rights and obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein shall survive the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.', creating uncertainty about obligations to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth after 5 years. This in-text contradiction happens due to the change of years in section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and shall remain in effect until terminated in writing by either party. Notwithstanding any such termination, the rights and obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein shall survive the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination timeframe of the Non-Disclosure Agreement from five (5) years to ten (10) years in section 3. The contradicted text in section 7 states that the obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information shall survive the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether the obligations related to confidential information disclosure continue indefinitely after the initial 5 or 10 years period, depending on which clause is prioritized. The original text set a definite timeframe of five years, after which certain obligations might terminate. The change to ten years creates uncertainty and a contradiction because section 7 states that the obligations survive 'any such termination', which now has two different possible termination dates. This is a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: CONSULTANT warrants and represents that CONSULTANT has bound its employees, agents and subcontractors to the terms and conditions of this Agreement or that each and every employee, agent or subcontractor has personally executed a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing terms and conditions no less stringent than the terms and conditions contained herein; and furthermore CONSULTANT hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss, cost, damage, expense or claim arising out of any breach of this provision or the failure of CONSULTANT or its employees, agents or subcontractors to protect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSULTANT warrants and represents that CONSULTANT has bound its employees, agents and subcontractors to the terms and conditions of this Agreement; however, subcontractors are exempt from the confidentiality obligations outlined herein; and furthermore CONSULTANT hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss, cost, damage, expense or claim arising out of any breach of this provision or the failure of CONSULTANT or its employees, agents or subcontractors to protect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original statement that the consultant has bound its subcontractors to the terms of the agreement, by adding an exemption for subcontractors. This creates uncertainty about who is bound by the confidentiality obligations. This in-text contradiction is created in the same section 6, with the added new condition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSULTANT warrants and represents that CONSULTANT has bound its employees, agents and subcontractors to the terms and conditions of this Agreement; however, subcontractors are exempt from the confidentiality obligations outlined herein; and furthermore CONSULTANT hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss, cost, damage, expense or claim arising out of any breach of this provision or the failure of CONSULTANT or its employees, agents or subcontractors to protect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that subcontractors are exempt from confidentiality obligations, while simultaneously requiring the consultant to indemnify NYNEX against any failure of subcontractors to protect Confidential Information. The original text stated that subcontractors were bound, while the new text creates an exception. The contradictory text is in the same location, creating a structural flaw as defined by the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and shall remain in effect until terminated in writing by either party. Notwithstanding any such termination, the rights and obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein shall survive the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and shall remain in effect until terminated in writing by either party. Upon written termination, all rights and obligations under this agreement cease immediately.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Notwithstanding any such termination, the rights and obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein shall survive the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.' the original text, now the inserted text explicitly states that ALL obligations cease upon written termination, which contradicts the original intent that confidentiality obligations survive termination. This change creates uncertainty about the duration of confidentiality obligations. This change contradicts the original definition of this same section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and shall remain in effect until terminated in writing by either party. Upon written termination, all rights and obligations under this agreement cease immediately.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that confidentiality obligations survive termination. The changed text explicitly states that ALL obligations cease upon written termination. This creates a direct conflict and contradiction within the document, specifically contradicting itself. The explanation confirms this contradiction, stating it creates uncertainty about the duration of confidentiality obligations and contradicts the original intent.

perturbation.original_text: No term or provision of this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be deemed waived, and no breach excused, unless such waiver or consent shall be in writing and signed by the party claimed to have waived or consented. Any consent by any party to, or waiver of, a breach by the other, whether express or implied, shall not constitute a consent to waiver of, or be cause for, any other, different or subsequent breach.
perturbation.changed_text: No term or provision of this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be deemed waived, and no breach excused, unless such waiver or consent shall be in writing and signed by the party claimed to have waived or consented. Any consent by any party to, or waiver of, a breach by the other, whether express or implied, shall constitute a consent to waiver of, or be cause for, any other, different or subsequent breach.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not' to 'shall', it introduces a contradiction to the original statement that Any consent by any party to, or waiver of, a breach by the other, whether express or implied, shall not constitute a consent to waiver of, or be cause for, any other, different or subsequent breach. With the text changed, there is now uncertainty of whether a breach is waived or not. This contradiction happens in the same section 9, due to the negation removal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No term or provision of this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be deemed waived, and no breach excused, unless such waiver or consent shall be in writing and signed by the party claimed to have waived or consented. Any consent by any party to, or waiver of, a breach by the other, whether express or implied, shall constitute a consent to waiver of, or be cause for, any other, different or subsequent breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that a waiver of one breach does not constitute a waiver of subsequent breaches. The changed text, by altering 'shall not' to 'shall', now states that a waiver of one breach *does* constitute a waiver of subsequent breaches. This directly contradicts the original intent and meaning of the clause, creating a clear in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the original text, where it clearly states that a waiver of one breach will not lead to a waiver of other breaches.
